title: rubus × semisuberectus
intro: Rubus × semisuberectus, also known as the semi-erect black raspberry, is a hybrid of the Rubus idaeus and Rubus occidentalis species, characterized by its semi-erect growth habit and edible black raspberry fruits. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues and skin problems. The health benefits of Rubus × semisuberus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Rubus × semisuberectus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in culinary and cosmetic applications.
